data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:39:08.270Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations have been problematic because the underlying Cosmos DB SDK is async-only. In earlier versions, EF Core allowed "sync-over-async" — it would block on async calls to provide synchronous APIs. 

In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), there was an opt-in to allow sync-over-async for the Cosmos DB provider. 

Now I'm trying to pin down the exact breaking change in .NET 11. The Cosmos DB provider has been moving away from supporting synchronous operations entirely, and I'm working through the timeline of when these changes happened — whether it was EF Core 9 that started deprecating sync-over-async, or if the full removal is coming in EF Core 11.

Given that the user mentioned having an opt-in configured in .NET 10, it seems like .NET 11 is where that opt-in gets removed completely, making any synchronous query methods throw an exception rather than silently working through the async wrapper.
